w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: PBI-02 — Design final PostgreSQL relational schema
As a developer, I want a properly designed PostgreSQL relational schema so that the system has a stable and normalized SQL data structure.
PBI-03 — Configure PostgreSQL locally + write team setup guide
As a developer, I want PostgreSQL configured locally and documented so that all team members can run the backend consistently
PBI-05 — Remove MongoDB packages, schemas, and environment variables
As a developer, I want MongoDB fully removed from the project so that the system relies solely on PostgreSQL.
PBI-06 — Migrate authentication system to PostgreSQL (JWT + hashing intact)
As a user, I want secure authentication using PostgreSQL-backed users so that my login works reliably after the database migration.
PBI-07 — Refactor Profile module to SQL
As a user, I want my profile data stored and retrieved from PostgreSQL so that my information persists reliably.
PBI-08 — Refactor Medications & Symptoms modules to SQL
As a patient, I want my medications and symptoms stored in SQL so that they are reliably saved and retrieved.
As a system administrator, I want role-based access control enforced so that users only access permitted resources.
PBI-10 — Create Doctor role schema and relationships
As a developer, I want a doctor role schema and doctor-patient relationship defined so that doctors can be assigned to patients.
PBI-11 — Build endpoint: Doctor views assigned patients
As a doctor, I want to see my assigned patients so that I can manage their medical data.
As a doctor, I want to view a patient’s aggregated medical history (symptoms + medications) so that I can understand their condition quickly.
PBI-13 — Finish remaining UI (Profile / Medications / Symptoms)
As a user, I want complete and responsive UI flows for profile, medications, and symptoms so that I can interact with the system easily.
As a patient, I want a functional dashboard connected to backend data so that I can see my medical summary.
As a user, I want to be redirected to the correct dashboard based on my role so that I land on the right page after login.
PBI-18 — Database migration testing + integrity validation
As a developer, I want to validate migration integrity so that no data relationships are broken.
PBI-19 — API testing (auth, profile, doctor routes)
As a developer, I want backend API routes tested so that authentication and doctor flows work reliably.
to make sure everything is connected and
PBI-04 — Integrate Prisma ORM and establish DB connection
Reason: ORM decision changed from Prisma to Sequelize.
(Doctor endpoints exist, but full dashboard aggregation endpoint not finalized.)
PBI-16 — Connect frontend to new SQL endpoints
(Connection partially implemented but not fully tested end-to-end.)
New PBIs Added After Sprint Backlog Submission
As a developer, I want backend API routes tested so that authentication and doctor flows work reliably.
As a user, I want stable authentication behavior so that role validation is consistent.
PBI-23 — Backend Test Coverage for Auth + Doctor Flows
As a developer, I want proper test coverage for auth and doctor flows so that regressions are prevented.
PBI-24 — Add Medication End Date Support
As a patient, I want to specify an end date for my medication so that the system can accurately track when my treatment finishes.
Successfully built a full-stack application using
Designed a professional, responsive UI with
Migrated the database from MongoDB to PostgreSQL using
Implemented and tested backend endpoints for doctor flow and medical history (end-to-end)
Added and validated role-based access control (doctor vs. patient)
Stabilized authentication and RBAC flows with proper token handling
Completed database schema setup and migrations successfully
Improved backend reliability through thorough testing and debugging
Early confusion about ORM choice (Prisma vs. Sequelize) affecting implementation direction
Git workflow was not clearly established at project start
Environment setup challenges (Docker, Postgres credentials, dotenv configuration mismatches)
Schema/model/controller misalignment caused repeated test failures
Role value inconsistencies led to incorrect authorization behavior
Some endpoints initially returned inconsistent user role data
Gaps in negative-case testing required additional rework
Merge conflicts due to multiple contributors modifying backend, auth, and middleware files
Start each PBI with a clear implementation plan before coding
Establish a documented development workflow (Git strategy, PR rules,
Agree early on one backend stack and define clear
Define database schema, API contracts, and RBAC expectations upfront
Standardize role constants in a single shared location to avoid mismatches
Test migrations immediately after schema updates
Implement proper error logging and improve automated test coverage (especially for authorization paths)
Reduce merge conflicts by using smaller PRs, merging frequently, keeping changes additive, and clarifying
Require a minimal UI contract (expected JSON structure) before backend implementation for dashboard features
Add a lightweight checklist for new protected endpoints (auth required, role check, negative tests,
